methods.method_name: Study Site Selection and Sample Collection
methods.description: Process for selecting study sites in the Wu river basin and collecting samples during the rainy season, avoiding rain days.

steps.description: Between May and June 2019, 5 study sites in the Wu river basin (2025 km 2 , 119 km long) in central Taiwan were visited (Fig. 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and visit 5 study sites in the Wu river basin, Taiwan.

steps.description: To avoid the dry period of the river, we collected all samples in the summer (rainy season), on days when it was not raining.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples during the summer rainy season on non-raining days.

steps.description: One sample was taken at site L0
steps.searchable_summary: Collect one sample at site L0.

steps.description: and three samples were collected at the remaining sites.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect three samples at each of the remaining sites (L1, L4, L5, L8).
outputs: Samples from sites L1, L4, L5, L8

methods.method_name: Larval Length and Dry Weight Measurement
methods.description: Procedure for cleaning, photographing, measuring length, drying, and weighing larval samples to determine dry weight.

steps.description: In order to mitigate contamination, the liquid used to clean equipment prior to use was filtered.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter cleaning liquid to mitigate contamination.

steps.description: All larvae were washed (especially visible fibers attached on body)
steps.searchable_summary: Wash larval samples, focusing on removing attached fibers.

steps.description: and photographed when each body length was recorded by computer software.
steps.searchable_summary: Photograph larvae and record body length using computer software.

steps.description: Three different genera (Thienemannimyia spp., Chironomus spp., and Orthocladius spp.) were detected in this area using a scanning electron microscope (SEM, Figs. S1 and S2) with a taxonomy key 34 , although not all larvae in our samples were identified further.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify larval genera using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and a taxonomy key.

steps.description: Vacuum filtration was conducted to briefly dry samples
steps.searchable_summary: Perform vacuum filtration to briefly dry samples.

steps.description: before we placed the filter papers (carefully covered by aluminum foil) inside a drying oven (approximately 42 °C, SM 400, Memmert) for at least 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry samples on filter papers in a drying oven at 42°C for at least 24 hours.

steps.description: We measured the weights of the filter papers with samples before and after drying using an analytical balance (GR-202 42 g/210 g, A&D company)
steps.searchable_summary: Measure weight of filter paper with sample before and after drying using an analytical balance.
Calculate dry weight from weights before and after drying.

steps.description: The dry samples were then placed inside a 150 ml bottle for further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Store dry samples in a 150 ml bottle.
